conditionShape: { field: string, operator: Operator, value?: any }
fieldConstraint: field must be a property declared on the target class (recursing through extends) AND must appear on the class's indexedFields list. Filtering on non-indexed fields is rejected server-side.

operators.name: Eq
operators.valueRule: scalar matching field's JSON Schema type

operators.name: Ne
operators.valueRule: scalar matching field's JSON Schema type

operators.name: Gt
operators.valueRule: scalar matching field's JSON Schema type

operators.name: Gte
operators.valueRule: scalar matching field's JSON Schema type

operators.name: Lt
operators.valueRule: scalar matching field's JSON Schema type

operators.name: Lte
operators.valueRule: scalar matching field's JSON Schema type

operators.name: In
operators.valueRule: array whose element type matches field's JSON Schema type

filterEndpoint.fieldsContract: fields is required. The backend returns only the listed top-level properties (plus _id, always). Naming a field that does not exist on the target class is rejected. Embedded sub-trees come back whole when their parent field is listed; no per-leaf projection inside an embedded class.
filterEndpoint.availableOn: top-level classes only — embedded classes are not independently queryable
